decisions.decision: Use WebSocket only, extending the existing WS infrastructure (RFC 6455 helpers, WS_CONNECTIONS registry, ws_broadcast pattern). No SSE.
alternatives_rejected: SSE (no existing infrastructure, ~150 lines of new transport code)
Both WS+SSE (unnecessary complexity, WS covers all requirements)

decisions.decision: Use global _WS_SEQ for transport-level ordering on all streams. Preserve per-market event journal seq in event payloads for event-level ordering and hash-chain integrity.
alternatives_rejected: Per-stream seq counters (adds complexity, no benefit since clients already handle interleaved global seq)

decisions.decision: No backfill-on-connect. Send initial snapshot on connect, then push live events. Clients use REST GET /v1/markets/{id}/events?fromSeq=N for gap recovery after reconnects.
alternatives_rejected: Backfill-from-seq on WS connect (adds ~60 lines of replay logic, duplicates REST pagination)

decisions.decision: Separate WS endpoints: /ws/markets/{id}/events for market event streams and /ws/accounts/{id}/risk for account risk streams. Existing /ws/markets/{id}/prices unchanged.
alternatives_rejected: Multiplexed single endpoint (mixes concerns, complex message schema)
Merged into price stream (different data shapes, update frequencies, scoping)

decisions.decision: Broadcast risk updates on every account state mutation (after sync_account_risk_state, sync_account_exposure_state, and settle_market_account_* calls). No threshold gating.
alternatives_rejected: Threshold-based broadcasting (adds complexity, may miss intermediate states)
Debounced broadcasting (delays feedback, event-sourced model expects completeness)

decisions.decision: No separate execution stream. The market event stream (CommandAccepted events) covers execution details. Clients filter by their own commandIds.
alternatives_rejected: Per-account execution stream (redundant with event journal, adds third stream type)

steps.task: Add WS_EVENT_CONNECTIONS and WS_RISK_CONNECTIONS registries in server.py, plus ws_register_events/ws_unregister_events and ws_register_risk/ws_unregister_risk helpers following the existing ws_register/ws_unregister pattern

Add ws_broadcast_events(market_id) function that broadcasts the latest event from the journal to all WS_EVENT_CONNECTIONS[market_id] subscribers, following the ws_broadcast pattern with _next_ws_seq() for transport sequencing
Add ws_broadcast_risk(account_id) function that broadcasts current ACCOUNT_RISK and ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE state to all WS_RISK_CONNECTIONS[account_id] subscribers
Insert ws_broadcast_events(market_id) call in emit_terminal_event() after the event is appended to the journal, so every new event is pushed to subscribers

Insert ws_broadcast_risk(account_id) calls at mutation points: after sync_account_risk_state() (line ~4355), after sync_account_exposure_state() (line ~4454), and after settle_market_account_risk/exposure (line ~3981-3982)

Add _handle_events_websocket(path) method to the HTTP handler that matches /ws/markets/{id}/events, performs WS upgrade, sends initial snapshot of recent events, then enters read loop. Follow _handle_websocket pattern exactly.

Add _handle_risk_websocket(path) method to the HTTP handler that matches /ws/accounts/{id}/risk, performs WS upgrade, sends initial risk+exposure snapshot, then enters read loop. Follow _handle_websocket pattern exactly.
Wire new WS handlers into do_GET: call _handle_events_websocket and _handle_risk_websocket before the existing _handle_websocket call
Add MarketEventMessage and AccountRiskMessage TypeScript interfaces to frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts
Add getMarketEventsWebSocketUrl(marketId) and getAccountRiskWebSocketUrl(accountId) URL helpers to frontend/src/lib/api/client.ts, following getMarketPricesWebSocketUrl pattern

Add useMarketEventSubscription(marketId) hook to frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts following useMarketPriceSubscription pattern: parse messages, update TanStack Query cache for market events, invalidate dependent queries
Add useAccountRiskSubscription(accountId) hook to frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts following useMarketPriceSubscription pattern: parse messages, update TanStack Query cache for account risk/exposure

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: estimated_total_lines=425 < 500 threshold. high_risk_steps=0 < 2 threshold. While there are 3 parallelizable groups, the backend steps (1-8) and frontend steps (9-12) share coordinated data shapes (event/risk message formats) and the total scope is moderate. All backend changes are in server.py, all frontend changes span 3 files. Sequential execution in one session is more efficient than spawning children for this scope.
